title: Verify retention and power-up sequencing
description: Confirm save and restore controls and supply sequencing keep state correct across power cycles.
body: Use when a domain uses retention registers to survive power-down. Do not assume retention works without exercising the exact save and restore control sequence. Actions: drive the save signal before power-down, sequence the supplies in the right order on wake, and compare restored state against the pre-power-down values. Validation signals include restored state matching the saved values and a power-up sequence that meets the supply ordering requirement. Risk boundary: a wrong sequence loses or corrupts retained state, so treat any mismatch after a cycle as a blocking defect.
